jokes.text: I thought I smelled a publicity man on the premises.
jokes.setup: Artie greets Norman warmly
jokes.punchline: Then immediately insults his profession with a smell metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie - fake warmth followed by industry cynicism. Perfect character voice and economy.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I thought I smelled a publicity man on the premises.

jokes.text: I remember the night they did Totie Fields. I think I heard every one-legged joke there ever was that night.
jokes.setup: Artie fondly remembering a roast
jokes.punchline: The 'fond' memory is of cruel disability jokes
jokes.explanation: Dark showbiz humor that reveals character while being genuinely shocking. The casual cruelty is the point.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I remember the night they did Totie Fields. I think I heard every one-legged joke there ever was that night.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, girl, if you're going to a roast, I gotta take you shopping, teach you how to dress.
jokes.setup: Beverly asks what to wear to the roast
jokes.punchline: Hank's over-the-top gay stereotype response
jokes.explanation: Standard Hank flamboyance, but feels a bit obvious and reliant on stereotype.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, my God, girl, if you're going to a roast, I gotta take you shopping, teach you how to dress.

jokes.text: I believe technically it's called roast mastering
jokes.setup: Artie says Hank wants to host
jokes.punchline: Hank's pretentious correction with fake technical term
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank - creating false expertise to sound important. The precision of 'technically' sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I believe technically it's called roast mastering

jokes.text: Come on! That whole stand-up thing is a... it's just over. I mean, 'have you noticed this,' and 'have you noticed that?'
jokes.setup: Hank dismissing Carl Reiner as old-fashioned
jokes.punchline: Reduces all observational comedy to a tired formula
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy about comedy itself, with Hank's typical overconfidence masking insecurity.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Come on! That whole stand-up thing is a... it's just over. I mean, 'have you noticed this,' and 'have you noticed that?'

jokes.text: Hillary Clinton may be Bill Clinton's first lady, but she is not his first lay.
jokes.setup: Hank demonstrating his 'superior' comedy skills
jokes.punchline: A hacky pun that proves Artie's point about him
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad joke that works because it reveals Hank's lack of talent while he thinks he's being brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Hillary Clinton may be Bill Clinton's first lady, but she is not his first lay.

jokes.text: See? I notice stuff, too. I just don't write it down and go to some moth-eaten nightclub and repeat it.
jokes.setup: Hank just told a terrible joke
jokes.punchline: His dismissal of professional comedy after proving he can't do it
jokes.explanation: Beautiful dramatic irony - Hank's complete lack of self-awareness is the real joke.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: See? I notice stuff, too. I just don't write it down and go to some moth-eaten nightclub and repeat it.

jokes.text: Get off the stage, ya fucking moron! I've taken shits more interesting than you are! You're nothin' but Larry Sanders' personal fuck-bitch!
jokes.setup: Hank asks Artie to heckle him to prove he can handle it
jokes.punchline: Artie's nuclear-level personal attack that goes way too far
jokes.explanation: Artie's volcanic response reveals his true feelings about Hank. The escalation from professional to deeply personal is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Get off the stage, ya fucking moron! I've taken shits more interesting than you are! You're nothin' but Larry Sanders' personal fuck-bitch!

jokes.text: Good comeback.
jokes.setup: Artie just destroyed Hank with vicious personal attacks
jokes.punchline: Artie's deadpan assessment as if it was just practice
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated follow-up that makes the previous outburst even funnier through contrast.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Good comeback.

jokes.text: You mean after his other best friend, Jeffrey Dahmer?
jokes.setup: Norman saying Dana can go third as Larry's best friend
jokes.punchline: Comparing Larry's friendship circle to serial killers
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor that cuts deep about Larry's social circle, though somewhat obvious reference.

dialogue.speaker: Dana
dialogue.line: You mean after his other best friend, Jeffrey Dahmer?

jokes.text: Jeffrey Dahmer is dead. He's unavailable. I know. That's the joke. What are you, in P.R?
jokes.setup: Norman misses Dana's Dahmer joke
jokes.punchline: Dana's incredulous response and PR insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the setup, and the PR dig works as industry insider humor.

dialogue.speaker: Dana
dialogue.line: Jeffrey Dahmer is dead. He's unavailable. I know. That's the joke.

dialogue.speaker: Norman
dialogue.line: What are you, in P.R?

jokes.text: I'm as free Saturday night as he is next month.
jokes.setup: Norman asking Bill to do the roast while trying to book Larry for Bill's show
jokes.punchline: Bill's quid pro quo delivered as wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel structure that reveals the transactional nature of Hollywood relationships.

dialogue.speaker: Bill Maher
dialogue.line: I'm as free Saturday night as he is next month.

jokes.text: all you gotta do is come back with a dick-related insult. That's it.
jokes.setup: Hank asking for heckler comeback advice
jokes.punchline: Reducing all comedy responses to one crude formula
jokes.explanation: Crude but honest about hack comedy techniques. Works as industry satire.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: all you gotta do is come back with a dick-related insult. That's it.

jokes.text: what if the heckler's a woman? Well, same thing. You talk about her dick. Makes 'em crazy.
jokes.setup: Hank asking about female hecklers
jokes.punchline: Phil's commitment to the dick-insult formula regardless of logic
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation of the dick-joke formula. The commitment to illogical advice is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: what if the heckler's a woman?

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Well, same thing. You talk about her dick. Makes 'em crazy.

jokes.text: I don't come down to your job and knock the dick outta your mouth while you're trying to work.
jokes.setup: Phil giving Hank another heckler comeback
jokes.punchline: Variation on classic 'your job' insult with sexual element
jokes.explanation: Classic insult structure but the sexual element feels forced rather than organic.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: I don't come down to your job and knock the dick outta your mouth while you're trying to work.

jokes.text: you can blow me like everyone else on table. At your table.
jokes.setup: Phil trying to be crude
jokes.punchline: His correction reveals he messed up the insult
jokes.explanation: The correction comedy works but the setup is weak and crude without purpose.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: you can blow me like everyone else on table. At your table.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. He told me he was going on vacation. Why do they have to lie that way?
jokes.setup: Learning Dennis Miller lied about being unavailable
jokes.punchline: Larry's innocent confusion about Hollywood dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry naivety about show business phoniness. Character-driven humor.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Wait a minute. He told me he was going on vacation. Why do they have to lie that way?

jokes.text: Dennis Miller passed. He's very sick. He's in the hospital. Christ! It's like he wants to be caught.
jokes.setup: Stevie lying about Dennis Miller being sick
jokes.punchline: His immediate escalation to hospital, then meta-commentary on his bad lying
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the lie plus self-aware commentary. Perfect Stevie character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Dennis Miller passed. He's very sick. He's in the hospital. Christ! It's like he wants to be caught.

jokes.text: Very simply, he's the guy we had to take to get Jerry fucking Seinfeld.
jokes.setup: Larry asking who Kip Addotta is
jokes.punchline: Artie's blunt explanation of package deals
jokes.explanation: Perfect industry insider humor and classic Artie directness. Economy is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Very simply, he's the guy we had to take to get Jerry fucking Seinfeld.

jokes.text: Is mosquito right? Shouldn't it be hung like a bee?
jokes.setup: Hank trying out his heckler comeback about being 'hung like a mosquito'
jokes.punchline: His confusion about which small insect to use
jokes.explanation: Hank's overthinking of a simple insult reveals his insecurity and lack of natural comedy instinct.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Is mosquito right? Shouldn't it be hung like a bee?

jokes.text: For 'the 2,000-year-old man.' We know that was my idea. Your idea was to tell Mel not to do it with a Jewish accent.
jokes.setup: Carl claiming Larry owes him for the 2,000-year-old man
jokes.punchline: The 'brilliant' contribution was removing the Jewish accent from a Jewish character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant industry insider joke about creative credit disputes and terrible notes. Very specific and earned.

dialogue.speaker: Carl Reiner
dialogue.line: For 'the 2,000-year-old man.' We know that was my idea. Your idea was to tell Mel not to do it with a Jewish accent.

jokes.text: Get me a damp cloth and a cyanide tablet.
jokes.setup: Hank dramatically declaring he won't attend if he can't host
jokes.punchline: His suicide request delivered with Victorian melodrama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank melodrama. The specific items requested make it funnier than generic death wish.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Get me a damp cloth and a cyanide tablet.

jokes.text: Sorry I couldn't make it to your roast, Lar, but I'm bone-fishin' with Barb. Get it? Bone? Barb? Fishin'?
jokes.setup: Dana doing George Bush impression at the roast
jokes.punchline: Bush explaining his own terrible pun
jokes.explanation: Solid impression work with the self-explanation being the joke, but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Dana Carvey (as George Bush)
dialogue.line: Sorry I couldn't make it to your roast, Lar, but I'm bone-fishin' with Barb. Get it? Bone? Barb? Fishin'?

jokes.text: Tell Hank to shut the fuck up.
jokes.setup: George Bush telegram
jokes.punchline: Even the President knows Hank is annoying
jokes.explanation: Good running gag setup. Economy is perfect and it builds the Hank-is-annoying theme.

dialogue.speaker: Dana Carvey
dialogue.line: Tell Hank to shut the fuck up.

jokes.text: shave my head with a cheese grater... get a barium enema, and check Dom DeLuise's ball sack for lumps.
jokes.setup: Johnny Carson's 'sealed envelope' message
jokes.punchline: Three increasingly horrible alternatives to attending Larry's roast
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure, each item more grotesque. The Dom DeLuise specificity is inspired.

dialogue.line: shave my head with a cheese grater... get a barium enema, and check Dom DeLuise's ball sack for lumps.

jokes.text: tell Hank that Ed said to shut the fuck up.
jokes.setup: Second celebrity telegram
jokes.punchline: Another 'shut up Hank' message, this time from Ed McMahon
jokes.explanation: Solid callback but diminishing returns. The pattern is established so impact lessens.

dialogue.line: tell Hank that Ed said to shut the fuck up.

jokes.text: I just wanna say to Bruno Kirby, you owe me 250 bucks, you little prick. Where is he? Oh, there he is. OK.
jokes.setup: Dana doing his roast set
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to address personal business
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - mixing personal grievances with professional performance. Very realistic.

dialogue.speaker: Dana Carvey
dialogue.line: I just wanna say to Bruno Kirby, you owe me 250 bucks, you little prick. Where is he? Oh, there he is. OK.

jokes.text: The great thing about Larry Sanders is that he never exploited the fact that he was a homosexual. He was gay, he was proud, but he didn't shove it down anybody's throat.
jokes.setup: Carl praising Larry for not exploiting his 'homosexuality'
jokes.punchline: The 'shoving down throat' double entendre
jokes.explanation: Obvious sexual double entendre. The setup is decent but the punchline is predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Carl Reiner
dialogue.line: The great thing about Larry Sanders is that he never exploited the fact that he was a homosexual. He was gay, he was proud, but he didn't shove it down anybody's throat.

jokes.text: Most of it hit you when he tripped over the microphone cord.
jokes.setup: Carl covered in confetti from Rip Taylor
jokes.punchline: Explaining it was accidental, not intentional showmanship
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback with understated delivery. The mundane explanation deflates the spectacle nicely.

dialogue.line: Most of it hit you when he tripped over the microphone cord.

jokes.text: oh, Christ, I'm not wastin' this on chicks!
jokes.setup: Why do gay men look better - do they become good-looking or vice versa?
jokes.punchline: The logical conclusion of not 'wasting' good looks on women
jokes.explanation: Good observational premise with solid punchline. The 'wasting' concept is genuinely funny.

dialogue.speaker: Carl Reiner
dialogue.line: oh, Christ, I'm not wastin' this on chicks!

jokes.text: He's doing his act. He's killing.
jokes.setup: Carl Reiner is supposed to be roasting Larry but is doing stand-up
jokes.punchline: Larry's recognition that he's being upstaged at his own roast
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry insecurity moment. The economy is excellent - two short phrases convey his entire emotional state.

dialogue.line: He's doing his act. He's killing.

jokes.text: luckily, the camera loves me.
jokes.setup: Carl describing having a camera put up his ass for medical exam
jokes.punchline: Applying showbiz terminology to medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant merger of showbiz ego with medical humiliation. The economy is perfect - maximum impact per word.

dialogue.speaker: Carl Reiner
dialogue.line: luckily, the camera loves me.

jokes.text: The bastard cancelled an hour ago.
jokes.setup: Larry asking where Seinfeld is
jokes.punchline: Artie's blunt revelation of the last-minute cancellation
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie directness cutting through Larry's expectations. The timing and bluntness work well.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: The bastard cancelled an hour ago.

jokes.text: Like today... we put salt on our asses... and we drove out to that petting zoo.
jokes.setup: Kip describing his 'relationship' with Larry
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and inappropriate sexual scenario
jokes.explanation: Genuinely bizarre and uncomfortable. Original but feels more shock-value than crafted humor.

dialogue.speaker: Kip Addotta
dialogue.line: Like today... we put salt on our asses... and we drove out to that petting zoo.

jokes.text: Well, then I owe Carrot Top a big apology for last year, don't I? Remember when I said he would suck a cock doing a sack race?
jokes.setup: Larry realizing roast jokes sound worse when they're about you
jokes.punchline: His specific cruel joke about Carrot Top
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's dawning self-awareness about his own cruelty.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, then I owe Carrot Top a big apology for last year, don't I? Remember when I said he would suck a cock doing a sack race?

jokes.text: and Tom Arnold's asshole was booked, so...
jokes.setup: Al thanking the hotel for hosting them last minute
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Tom Arnold's rectum was the first choice venue
jokes.explanation: Solid insult with good economy. The venue/body part parallel works well structurally.

dialogue.line: and Tom Arnold's asshole was booked, so...

jokes.text: That's the kind of quick wit and jest that keeps you on Larry's couch.
jokes.setup: Hank making a quick interjection
jokes.punchline: Carl suggesting Hank's 'wit' is why he's homeless/couchsurfing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double insult - Hank's comedy isn't funny AND he's homeless. Great roast technique.

dialogue.speaker: Carl Reiner
dialogue.line: That's the kind of quick wit and jest that keeps you on Larry's couch.

jokes.text: you are absolutely adequate.
jokes.setup: Norm thanking Carl Reiner
jokes.punchline: Damning with extremely faint praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and word choice. 'Adequate' is a devastating non-compliment delivered with false sincerity.

dialogue.speaker: Norm Crosby
dialogue.line: you are absolutely adequate.

jokes.text: When I first met Larry Sanders, he was screwing my cousin. And I didn't even know that he knew Harvey.
jokes.setup: Larry was screwing his cousin
jokes.punchline: Reveal that the cousin is male (Harvey)
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke with good economy. Professional roast material.

dialogue.speaker: Norm Crosby
dialogue.line: When I first met Larry Sanders, he was screwing my cousin. And I didn't even know that he knew Harvey.

jokes.text: When I first met Larry Sanders, he was screwing my cousin. And I didn't even know that he knew Harvey.
jokes.setup: Standard roast opening about Larry screwing someone
jokes.punchline: The 'someone' is male cousin Harvey
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection structure works well. The Harvey reveal is effective though somewhat predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Norm Crosby
dialogue.line: When I first met Larry Sanders, he was screwing my cousin. And I didn't even know that he knew Harvey.

jokes.text: He's on that new Hollywood agent diet. You can only eat while you're being eaten.
jokes.setup: Larry looks great on a new diet
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'eating' vs 'being eaten alive' by the industry
jokes.explanation: Excellent industry-specific wordplay. The diet/destruction parallel is clever and well-structured.

dialogue.speaker: Norm Crosby
dialogue.line: He's on that new Hollywood agent diet. You can only eat while you're being eaten.

jokes.text: You just... never seem to be able to get 'em at home.
jokes.setup: Similarity between eggs Benedict and oral sex
jokes.punchline: Both are restaurant/outside-the-home experiences
jokes.explanation: Solid parallel structure joke. Familiar territory but well-executed comparison.

dialogue.speaker: Norm Crosby
dialogue.line: You just... never seem to be able to get 'em at home.

jokes.text: I'd rather have Al Gore fuck me in the ass.
jokes.setup: Al as President Clinton recommending Larry for White House dinner
jokes.punchline: Clinton's alleged preference for anal sex with VP over Larry's comedy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and political specificity. The Gore reference adds absurdist element to the insult.

dialogue.speaker: President Clinton
dialogue.line: I'd rather have Al Gore fuck me in the ass.

jokes.text: What the fuck am I doing here? I'm an actor. We do 2 different things. Comedians jerk off. I work.
jokes.setup: Bruno supposed to roast Larry
jokes.punchline: Instead complains about being there and insults all comedians
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anti-roast moment. Bruno's genuine hostility creates uncomfortable laughs.

dialogue.speaker: Bruno Kirby
dialogue.line: What the fuck am I doing here? I'm an actor. We do 2 different things. Comedians jerk off. I work.

jokes.text: And I don't owe you $250, pal. You owe me $250.
jokes.setup: Callback to Dana's earlier complaint about Bruno owing money
jokes.punchline: Bruno's angry counter-accusation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the personal business interruption theme. Creates real discomfort.

dialogue.speaker: Bruno
dialogue.line: And I don't owe you $250, pal. You owe me $250.

jokes.text: The key lime pie is delicious. This is the worst fucking night of my life. And that was chocolate cake.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to find positives/Artie's harsh reality
jokes.punchline: Even Larry's attempt at optimism is wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Larry's terrible night - even his positive observation is factually wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: The key lime pie is delicious.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: This is the worst fucking night of my life.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And that was chocolate cake.

jokes.text: This is the worst fucking night of my life. And that was chocolate cake.
jokes.setup: Larry having a miserable time at his own roast
jokes.punchline: Even the key lime pie (his favorite) was wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - catastrophizing while focusing on small details. Great callback structure.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This is the worst fucking night of my life. And that was chocolate cake.

jokes.text: as opposed to Larry who's just old.
jokes.setup: Praising Carl Reiner for bringing lifetime of laughter
jokes.punchline: Larry just has the age without the accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure. The contrast between 'lifetime of laughter' and 'just old' is sharp.

dialogue.speaker: Jon Stewart
dialogue.line: as opposed to Larry who's just old.

jokes.text: hey, why don't you speak up? Only the people at your table know you're an asshole.
jokes.setup: Jon Stewart making jokes about Larry
jokes.punchline: Hank using his rehearsed heckler comeback inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the heckler training, used completely inappropriately. Perfect Hank moment.

dialogue.speaker: Jon Stewart
dialogue.line: hey, why don't you speak up? Only the people at your table know you're an asshole.

jokes.text: I don't come to your job and knock the dick out of your ass.
jokes.setup: Hank continuing his inappropriate heckling
jokes.punchline: Butchering the comeback he was taught
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback execution - Hank screwing up the rehearsed line makes it funnier and more pathetic.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I don't come to your job and knock the dick out of your ass.

jokes.text: You'll either behave yourself, or I'm gonna snap your neck like a butter bean.
jokes.setup: Artie threatening Hank to control his behavior
jokes.punchline: The specific 'butter bean' comparison for violence
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie violence with unexpectedly specific vegetable comparison. The butter bean detail is inspired.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You'll either behave yourself, or I'm gonna snap your neck like a butter bean.

jokes.text: It's a plate for bulimics. See, they can eat...
jokes.setup: Carrot Top showing a prop plate
jokes.punchline: Designed specifically for eating disorder
jokes.explanation: Typical Carrot Top prop comedy. Dark subject matter but lacks sophistication in execution.

dialogue.speaker: Carrot Top
dialogue.line: It's a plate for bulimics. See, they can eat...

jokes.text: everyone I love is here tonight, and, um... what an honor.
jokes.setup: Larry's emotional speech about everyone he loves being present
jokes.punchline: The pause before 'what an honor' suggests he's realizing how few people that actually is
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending beat. The pause suggests Larry's dawning realization about his loneliness, delivered with subtle pathos.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: everyone I love is here tonight, and, um... what an honor.
